samples.gvd:
module JC_counter (
    input                clk,
    input                rst_n,
    output reg [63:0]     Q
);

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (~rst_n) begin
        
        Q <= 64'b0;
    end else begin
        
        if (Q[0] == 1'b0) begin
            Q <= {1'b1, Q[63:1]};
        end else begin
            Q <= {1'b0, Q[63:1]};
        end
    end
end
endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="JC_counter">
    <description>64-bit Johnson Counter</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal to initialize the counter.</input>
        <output id="Q" width_description="[63:0]">64-bit register representing the current count value.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (negedge rst_n). If the reset signal is active (rst_n == 0), the counter value (Q) is initialized to 0. Otherwise, based on the least significant bit (Q[0]), the counter either increments or decrements its value by shifting the bits to the right and appending a 1 or 0 at the most significant bit position.</implementation>
</module>
